title: Fully understanding Eva
wikitext:
[]: Fully understanding Eva             Quote Post by V » Mon Aug 11, 2008 9:42 pm I increasingly think it is impossible for someone to be able to FULLY, and I mean FULLY, understand the series who was not living in Japan in 1996. I mean...."The Lord of the Rings" wasn't meant for an immediate audience. Granted, it was written by a World War I veteran and sort of spoke to the "Lost Generation" of the Hemingways, etc. nonetheless it was a work which was removed from modern references or even like satirical jabs. I'm not talking about the high and important goal of "understanding the series' themes" actually :) :) haha, and YES there are many who say that you had to be THERE in Japan at that moment in social history (i.e. reconstruction from World War II is over and we are the children of the generation that did all the reconstruction and now we're just a bunch of emasculated children with psychological issues, etc. etc.) ******What I'm talking about right now, is I've realized it is increasingly impossible to get ALL of the little pop-culture references unless you were living in Japan in the 90's. By which I mean, in the great way that Tolkien did this with LOTR, the Gainax guys are obsessive fans themselves, and "paint in the corners". every great fantasy/scifi world does this. So what Gainax did was they "paint in the corners" of every frame. You know, when you pause it to read what magazines Misato has in her apartment or whatever. And right now, I'm rewatching episode 11 and I see all sorts of soda vending machines..........and I've heard that there's a subtle joke that in the Eva-verse, all of the soda you see is mostly old brands that actually got cancelled and that they don't make anymore but that the Gainax guys like. Sort of like if right now you were to go "wow, I used to love "Mr Pib" but they don't make it anymore. And I'm working on a TV series set 20 years in the future: wouldn't it be funny to subtly animate in cans of "Mr Pib" in vending machines, establishing that it "comes back" 20 years from now?" So I'm vaguely aware that there are little pop culture references and jokes like that (the soda in vending machines being from popular but canceled flavors), but increasingly I doubt that it is IMPOSSIBLE to catch them all: even if one were to acquire knowledge of Japanese writing, these are old brand names only used in Japan itself. It would be like asking ME to know what an obscure soda flavor was in Australia 20 years ago (do those come in giant cans too?)...and Australia speaks English! I mean are there fansites for Eva in Japan or what? They'd keep track of such a thing. I've tried searching around but I can't find anything directly comparable. Even Germany and Russia still have active Eva fansites.

[]:             Quote Post by swamp147 » Mon Aug 11, 2008 10:23 pm Good point, I never thought of it this way. The only time this has crossed my mind (barely, just a little thought that obviously wasn't like your post) was when I was watching Eva for the first time, and when Shinji went to Rei's apartment to give her her new card, and he rang the doorbell (at least that's what I think it is, it also lets you speak into it so you can say who you are or w/e), and the doorbell thing had "Pin Pom" written on it. I always wondered what that was. I assumed it was a popular brand or company that made such doorbells. There are probably some other things that I wondered, and I just assumed I didn't know because they were the culture in Japan (which I'm not too familiar with). Edit - Oh yeah, I wondered if it was commonplace to do your laundry at a laundromat in a city, instead of owning a washing machine. Not that this is an exemplary example of the things you mentioned in your post, just slightly related.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Mon Aug 11, 2008 10:46 pm I mean one thing I DO know is that in the TV series, they drink "UCG canned coffee"...and the "G" is written practically like another "G" the real life brand "UCC canned coffee" is what Anno and the other Gainax guys drank and NOW, Rebuild of Eva is having lots of promotional tie ins.....it reminds me of how Star Wars Episode I had a HUGE marketing tie-in blitz with Pizza Hut/Taco Bell/KFC (all are owned by the same company).......except Star Wars is in a "galaxy far away" while Eva is "20 years from now" so they can easily do product placement....... ......anyway, in Rebuild, they just openly have product placement of the REAL "UCC canned coffee" kind of also how they actually use "Yebisu" beer again in Rebuild (infamously, in the series they started out using "Yebisu" but the network Eva was airing on objected to it because Yebisu wasn't actually paying them money, Gainax just thought it would be fun to use them......so they had to switch to the fictional "Yebichu" brand.....though they changed that back in the DVD release)

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Aug 12, 2008 5:23 am V is very right here, but it doesn't effect only pop culture references You CAN'T "get" Rei's immense popularity if you don't understand state of Japan in 1995

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Tue Aug 12, 2008 1:31 pm Very true, V.

[]: Re: Its impossible for anyone to fully "get" Eva w             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Aug 12, 2008 6:59 pm V wrote: I increasingly think it is impossible for someone to be able to FULLY, and I mean FULLY, understand the series who was not living in Japan in 1996.... What I'm talking about right now, is I've realized it is increasingly impossible to get ALL of the little pop-culture references unless you were living in Japan in the 90's.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Aug 12, 2008 7:14 pm Interesting point. The only thing I have to add though is just because you don't fully understand/"get" something doesn't mean you can't enjoy it.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Aug 12, 2008 7:45 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: The only thing I have to add though is just because you don't fully understand/"get" something doesn't mean you can't enjoy it.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Wed Aug 13, 2008 8:49 am It is possible to understand even if you aren't living in 1996. (Well, it may not be totally comprehendable no matter what) You just have to looke at the history, the politics, and whatever was going on in 1996 at the time, (or is this easy to say for me, because I was in Japan in 1996-1997?)

[]:             Quote Post by V » Wed Aug 13, 2008 9:25 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: Interesting point. The only thing I have to add though is just because you don't fully understand/"get" something doesn't mean you can't enjoy it.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Wed Aug 13, 2008 11:20 am I think that is the whole point of the discussions, and trying to solve what makes Eva, Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Aug 13, 2008 4:42 pm The Imperialist wrote: (or is this easy to say for me, because I was in Japan in 1996-1997?)

[]:             Quote Post by Zerogoki » Wed Aug 13, 2008 5:27 pm "I Think I'm Turning Japanese" is about masturbation?? I always assumed that there would be some pop-culture references in Eva that I wouldn't get, as there are with most animes and mangas that I enjoy. To me it just adds to the fun of watching/reading it, as I can then go online and learn something I didn't know before. @ Swampy: I always wondered about the PinPom thing as well. I sort of figured that it's the noise the doorbell makes (doi!), but as for a take on an actual brand name? I feel a little digging around coming on...

[]:             Quote Post by V » Wed Aug 13, 2008 5:41 pm Zerogoki wrote: "I Think I'm Turning Japanese" is about masturbation??

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Aug 13, 2008 6:54 pm V wrote: What do your eyes do at the height of orgasm?

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Wed Aug 13, 2008 7:03 pm whoa, hey, wait, WTF is going on here? are we even allowed to go there?

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Wed Aug 13, 2008 8:50 pm @ Ornette Let's just say, a little bit. Little.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Aug 14, 2008 8:51 am V may have come up with a bad example, but she's correct. Knowing the lyrics' meaning really changes the effect the song has on you. Problems with the time period? The same thing affects a whole lot of "universal" movies, including 2001: A Space Odyssey. There always are a few references that fit one particular time period (Product placement, like V said). And like Xard said, the popularity of Rei was due to the conditions prevalent in 1995. Shinji's collapse into depression after a period where things began to look up would have resonated with viewers in a country plunged deep into recession.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Thu Aug 14, 2008 9:19 am But it is possible to think about the 'mentality' of the period if we pay close attention to the socioeconomic goings at the time. Or is it more difficult than I imagine it to be?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Thu Aug 14, 2008 10:42 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: V wrote: What do your eyes do at the height of orgasm?
summary: []: Fully understanding Eva             Quote Post by V » Mon Aug 11, 2008 9:42 pm I increasingly think it is impossible for someone to be able to FULLY, and I mean FULLY, understand the series who was not living in Japan in 1996. I mean...."The Lord of the Rings" wasn't meant for an immediate audience. Granted, it was written by a World War I veteran and sort of spoke to the "Lost Generation" of the Hemingways, etc.
